author: William Lilly
title: Judging Querent's Lifespan from Afflictions
text: violent influence or nature of the Planet afflicting, or nature of the Lord of the eighth or sixth house, then you may judge the Querent is not long lived, but near some danger, or shall undergo some misfortune in one kind or other, according to the quality of the significator and signification of that or those houses they are Lords of.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents: Lord of Ascendant
text: You must see if the Lord of the Ascendant be going to Combustion, or to the Sun or Lord of the eighth or fourth, how many degrees he is distant from the Sun, or Lord of the eighth or fourth, and in what Signe either of them are in; if the space betwixt them be eight degrees, and in a common Signe, it denotes so many months; if in a fixed Signe, so many years; if in movable, so many weeks: this is only for example, all manner of Questions. 113 example, and in general; for the measure of time must be limited according to the other significators concurring in judgment herein.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents: Moon's Distance
text: Secondly, having considered the Lord of the Ascendant, see how many degrees the Moon is also distant from any Infortune, or from the Lords of the sixth or eighth, and in what Signe or Signes, their Nature, Quality and House wherein they are placed.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents: Infortune in Angles
text: Thirdly, consider if there be an Infortune in the Ascendant, how many degrees the Cusp of the house wants of that degree the unfortunate Planet is in, or if the unfortunating Planet be in the seventh, how many degrees the Ascendant wants of his true Opposition, and compute the time of Death, Sickness or Misfortune according to the dimension of degrees in Signes movable, common, or fixed.

author: William Lilly
title: Sickness from Lord of Sixth
text: If you find the Lord of the Ascendant afflicted most of all by the Lord of the sixth, and in the sixth, or if the Lord of the Ascendant come to Combustion in the sixth, you may judge the Querent will have very many and tedious sicknesses, which will scarce leave him till his death; and the more certain your judgment will be, if the Lord of the Ascendant, and Lord of the eighth and the Moon be all placed in the sixth.

author: William Lilly
title: Death from Lord of Eighth
text: If you find the Lord of the Ascendant, the Sign ascending, or Moon most principally impedited or unfortunated by the Lord of the eighth, or that Planet who afflicts your significators out of the eighth, then you may judge that the Sickness with which he is now afflicted, or is shortly to be troubled withal, will end him, and that his death is approaching or that death is threatened.

author: William Lilly
title: Misfortune from Other House Lords
text: But if you find that the Lord of the Ascendant, or Signe of the Ascendant, or the Moon are chiefly afflicted by the Lords of some other houses, you shall judge his misfortune from the nature of the house or houses whereof the Planet or Planets afflicting are Lords; and the first original thereof, or discovery, shall be signified from something, Man or Woman, &c. belonging to that house wherein you find the Planet afflicting posited, and thereby you shall judge a misfortune and not death.

author: William Lilly
title: Effects of Planetary Natures
text: The fixed Stars mentioned, being of the nature of Mercury, show sudden distempers of body, or Fevers, Murders, Quarrels, &c. of the nature of Saturn, quartan Agues, Poverty, casual hurts by Falls; &c. of the nature of Mars, they declare Consumptions, Madness, cozenage by false Evidence or Writings; of the nature of the Moon, Tumults, Commotions, Wind-chollick, danger by Water, &c. of the nature of the Sun, envy of Magistrates, hurt in the Eyes, &c. of the nature of Jupiter, oppression by domineering Priests, or by some Gentleman; of the nature of Venus, then prejudice by some Woman, the Pox, or Cards, Dice and Wantonness.

author: William Lilly
title: Caution in Predicting Death
text: You must carefully avoid pronouncing Death rashly, and upon one bare testimony; you must observe, though the Lord of the Ascendant be going to Combustion, whether either Jupiter or Venus cast not some Sextile or Trine to the Lord of the Ascendant, ere he come to perfect Combustion, or any other infortune, for that is an argument that either Medicine or strength of Nature will contradict that malignant influence, or take off part of that misfortune; but when you find two or more of the rules aforesaid concurring to death, you may be more bold in your Judgment: yet concerning the absolute time of death of any party, I have found it best to be wary, and have as much as I could, refrained this manner of judgment; only thus much by the Question may be known, that if you find the significators, as aforesaid, afflicted, you may judge the man or party inquiring to be no long lived man, or subject to many miseries and calamities, and this I know by many verified examples: the knowledge hereof is of excellent use for such as would purchase any Lease or Office, or thing for Life or Lives, &c. or for those who would carefully in a natural Way prevent those casualties their natures or inclinations would run them into.

author: William Lilly
title: Quarters of Heaven Defined
text: You must know that the twelve Houses are divided into the East, West, North and South quarters of Heaven. The Cusp of the first House is the beginning of the East, and it's called the East Angle, from the Degree of the first house to the Degree or Cusp of the tenth House or Medium Coeli, containing the 12, 11, and tenth Houses, are East, inclining to the South: from the Cusp of the tenth House to the Cusp of the seventh House, containing the 9, 8, and 7, is South, verging towards the West: from the degree of the seventh House to the Cusp of the fourth House, consisting of the 6, 5, and fourth houses, is the West, tending to the North: from the Degree of the fourth House to the Degree of the Ascendant, containing the 3, 2, and first Houses, is North inclining to the West.

author: William Lilly
title: Directing Affairs by Quarters
text: Having viewed the several quarters of Heaven, see in which of them you find the Planet that promiseth the Querent most good, and where you find Jupiter, Venus, Moon or Part of Fortune, or two or more of them, to that quarter direct your affairs; and if you have the Part of Fortune and the Moon free from Combustion and other misfortunes, go that ways, or to that quarter of heaven where you find her; for you must consider, that though Jupiter and Venus be Fortunes, yet casually they may be Infortunes, when they are Lords of the 8, 12, or 6: in that case you must avoid the quarter they are in, and observe the Sun and the Moon and Lord of the Ascendant; and as near as you can avoid that quarter of Heaven where the infortunes are, especially when they are significators of mischief, otherways either Jupiter or Saturn being Lord of the Ascendant or second House, tenth or eleventh, may (being essentially strong) prove friendly. The general way of resolving this Question is thus: If the Querent does only desire to live where he may enjoy most health, look in what Signe and quarter of Heaven the Lord of the Ascendant and Moon are in, and which of them are strongest, and does cast his or her more friendly Aspect to the Degree ascending; to that quarter of Heaven repair for Healths sake: If the Querent desire to know to what part he may steer his course for obtaining of an Estate or Fortune, then see where and in what quarter of Heaven the Lord of the second is placed, and the Part of Fortune, and his Dispositor or two of them; for where and in what quarter they are best fortified, from thence may he expect his most advantage, &c. Of this shall speak casually in subsequent Judgments.

author: William Lilly
title: Best Periods of Life
text: See either in what Angle or quarter of Heaven the fortunate and promising Planets are posited in; for in this way of Judicature, we give usually to every house five years, but sometimes more or less, according as you see the significators promising Life or Death, (but commonly five years we give) beginning with the twelfth, and so to the eleventh, then the tenth, then the ninth, &c. and so to the Ascendant; as if in your Question you find Jupiter or Venus in the eleventh or tenth house, you may judge the Man or Woman to have lived happily from the fifth year of his age to the fifteenth, or in his youth: if they, or either of them, be in the eighth or seventh, they declare that from twenty to thirty he will, or has lived, and may live contentedly: if Jupiter or Venus be in the 6, 5, or 4, then judge after his middle age, or from 30 to 45, he may do very well: if you find Jupiter or Venus in third, second or first, then his best days, or his greatest happiness will be towards his old age, or after he is forty five until sixty; if you find the significators of Life very strong, and signifying long Life, you may add one year to every house, for so that it's possible the Querent may live more than sixty years, or until seventy, or more, as many we know do.

author: William Lilly
title: Separation and Application for Events
text: Lastly, you must observe at the time of your Question, how the Lord of the Ascendant and the Moon are separated, from what Planet, and by what aspect; the separation of those show the manner of Accidents which have preceded the Question; their next application, what in future may be expected; if you consider what house or houses, the Planet or Planets they separated from are Lords of, it acquaints you with the matter, nature, person and quality of the thing already happened: ill, if the aspect was ill; Good, if the aspect was good; and if you observe the quality of the next aspect by application, and the well or ill being and position of the Planet or Planets applied unto, it delivers the quality of the next succeeding Accidents and Casualties, their nature, proportion, time when they will happen or come upon the Querent.

author: William Lilly
title: Eye Blemish from Benefics in Angles
text: If Jupiter and Venus in the South, Angle, in a fiery sign, a South-east Sign; being in square or opposition of the Sun, the querent has some blemish or the like near one of his Eyes; and this is ever true, if the square or opposition be in Angles, and either of them have any ill.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: General Use
text: Ptolemy does not more consider a Planet than the Part of Fortune, thus charactered Part of Fortune; it has no aspect, but any Planets may cast their aspect unto it. The greatest use of it, that hitherto I have either read or made of it for, is thus; That if we find it well placed in the heaven, in a good house, or in a good aspect of a Benevolent Planet, we judge the Fortune or estate of the querent to be correspondent unto its strength, viz. if it be well posited or in an Angle or in those signs wherein it's fortunated, we judge the estate of the querent to be sound and firm, if Part of Fortune is otherways placed, we do the contrary.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Calculation
text: The manner either night or day to take it thus: First, consider the Signe, degree and minute of the Moon. Secondly, the Signe, degree and minute of the Sun. Thirdly, subtract the place of the Sun from the Moon, by adding twelve Signes to the Moon if you cannot do it otherways; what remains, reserve and add to the Signe and degree of the Ascendant; if both added together make more than twelve Signes, cast away twelve, and what Signes, degrees and minutes remain, let your Part of Fortune be there. Whether your Figure be by day or night, observe this Method; for how many degrees the Sun is distant from the Moon, so many is the Part of Fortune from the Ascendant; but because this may not be thoroughly understood by every Learner upon a sudden, let him observe this general rule, the better to guide him. If the Part of Fortune be taken upon a new Moon, it will be in the Ascendant. If upon the first quarter, in the fourth house. If upon the full Moon, in the seventh house. If upon the last quarter, in the tenth house. After the change, and before the first quarter, you shall ever have her in the first, second or third house. After the first quarter until the full Moon, in the fourth, fifth or sixth. After the full Moon until the last quarter, in the seventh, eighth or ninth houses. After the last quarter, either in the tenth, eleventh or twelfth. So that if the Learner does mistake, he may by this method easily see his error; ever remembering, that the more days are passed after the change or quarter, &c. the more remote the Part of Fortune is from the Angle preceding. Some have used to take Part of Fortune in the night from the Moon to the Sun: which if you do, you must then make the place of the Sun your first place, and add the Ascendant as in the former method. Ptolemy, day and night takes it as above directed, with whom all Practicioners at this day consent.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Dignities & Debilities
text: Here follows a Table, by help whereof you may examine the strength of Part of Fortune in any Figure you erect. The Part of Fortune, wherein if it be, it has allowed dignities: In the Signs Leo, Gemini, Sagittarius, in these Signs strong and fortunate. In this Sign fortunate Scorpio, so it be in the Terms of Jupiter or Venus. Aspecting with Jupiter or Venus it has dignities. In Trine with Jupiter or Venus. So it be in an Angle. At first or Tenth, it has allowed dignities. Part of Fortune is strong by being in houses: Seventh, Fourth, Eleventh, Second or Fifth. Ninth. With Regulus 24.34. Aspecting with any of the Fixed Stars. Not Combust, or under the Sun's Beams. The Part of Fortune is weak in these signs: Cancer, Virgo, Pisces, being in any of these signs it has debilities. In Libra, it neither gets or loses. Saturn or Mars has debilities. In opposition with Saturn or Mars. If or aspects in opposition of Saturn or Mars. In Terms of Saturn or Mars. See it in Houses: Viz. in the sixth. With Caput Algol 26.48. There are many other Parts which the Arabians have mentioned frequently in their Writings, of which we make very little use in this Age: I shall, as occasion offers, teach the finding them out, and what they said, they did signify: sometimes the Part of Fortune has signification of Life, and sometimes of Sickness; which occasionally I shall teach, as matter and occasion offer, adhering to the true observation of the Ancients: but I am little hitherto satisfied concerning Part of Fortune its true effects; intending to take pains therein hereafter, and in my intentions.

author: William Lilly
title: Signs of death or near death
text: In judging this Question, see if whether the Lord of the Afcendant, the Moon, and Lord of the eight House or Planet in the eighth house be corporally joined together; or that the Moon, Lord of the Afcendant and Lord of the eight are in opposition either in the eighth and second, or twelfth and sixth, for these are arguments the party is deceased, or sick, and very near death. See also if there be any translation of the light of the Lord of the Afcendant unto the Lord of the eight, especially in degrees deep, lame or deficient; or on the contrary, that there be any translation or carrying the virtue or influence of the Lord of the eight unto the Lord of the Afcendant; or if, the Lord of the eight be posited in the Afcendant, or if the Lord of the Afcendant and the Moon be placed in the fourth House, these are testimonies the party absent is dead.

author: William Lilly
title: Past troubles of an absent person
text: If the Lord of the Afcendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the sixth, you may say the absent hath been lately sick; if from the Lord of the eight, he hath been in danger of death, but is not dead; hath been lately much troubled in mind, in fear of imprisonment, arrests, &c. if from the Lord of the second, he hath been hard put to it for money, or in distress for want; if from the Lord of the seventh, in some quarrel or contention; if from the Lord of the ninth or third, he hath been crossed in his journey (if he was at Sea by contrary winds, or Pirates) if at hand by Thieves, bad Ways, &c. and so of the rest.

author: William Lilly
title: Predicting news of an absent person
text: So if you find the absent alive, and you would know, when happily you may hear of him; see in your Ephemerides when the Lord of the eleventh and Lord of the Afcendant come to a Trine or Sextile Aspect, and about that time, if not that day, news will be had of him; or if the Moon apply to a Sextile or Trine of the Lord of the Afcendant, see how many degrees she wants of the Aspect, and give days, weeks or months, viz. For every degree in moveable Signs a day, in common Signs weeks, in fixed Signs months.

author: William Lilly
title: Significators for children, kin, strangers
text: The matter quesited after must be required from that house which signifieth Children, and that is the fifth, considered who is Lord of the Sign. You must have taken signification from the Moon, who is Lord of the third house; for you may see in the signification of the Houses, page 52. that the third house signifieth Brethren, Sisters, Kindred and Neighbours - you might safely have adjudged; she should have found any one of these at home, because their Significator is in an Angle. But if she had enquired, Whether she should have found a person to whom she had no relation but a Party at home, yea or not, as to a stranger; then the Lord of the seventh, had been his Significator, whom I find to be in the Sign of Libra, and in the second house of Heaven, for wanting more than five degrees of the Cusp of the third, he is not admitted to have signification in that house.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent person's location and distance
text: In the first place therefore, I find Sun in the second house, and in a Northern quarter of Heaven (for from the Cusp of the fourth house to the Cusp of the first, or Afcendant, is so, as you may see page 48). Next I find, Mars who is Significator of the Party with whom she would speak with, is in Aries; which is an Easterly Sign, as you may see Page 97. Being Mars is not in an Angle, I say he is not at home; the second house wherein he is being a Succedant, I say he is not far from home. The quarter of Heaven wherein at present he is is North-east, for so Sign and quarter import. His distance from home may be a Furlong or two, because his Significator is in a Succedant house. The quality of the place or ground where you may expect to find him, must be judged from the Sign wherein Mars his Significator is, viz. Aries, a fiery Sign. What manner of place that Sign signifies, see in page 98, and you shall there find, it represents in the Fields, Hills, or Grounds that rise a little; so that the Party enquired after being absent, you must direct a messenger to find him, in such or such a Ground, or part of the Ground, as is of the nature and quality described, and butting or lying to that quarter of Heaven, as is formerly directed, viz. North-east. But had it been so, that you were informed, the man was in a Town, and not in the Fields, then enquire in the Town for Mars.

author: William Lilly
title: Judging moles and marks
text: as masculine, so is the Sign Aries; and as the later degrees of Aries ascend, so the Querent confessed a Mole on the lower part of her Reins, towards the Hanches; Venus being the Sign of the sixth, shewed she had one on the Forehead, near the Hair, for you see the Cusp of the house is but four degrees; Jupiter Lord of Venus, being in a masculine Sign, viz. in Gemini, but under the earth, shewed a Mole on the right Thigh, towards the middle of it, on the back part, or that part which is not visible; the Moon being in Sagittarius, 26 deg, 43 Min, in a Feminine Sign, and under the Earth, I told her she had one Mole under her Foot, towards the extremity of her left Foot. The Quesited party being her Son, had Gemini the ninth degree for his Afcendant, which denoted a Mole on the left side of his Cheek; and as Mercury signifies the Foot, so he had one on the left Foot, a little below the Ancle, for you see few degrees ascend. The sixth house from the fifth, is the tenth in the Figure, where you see Mars, which signified, that near his right Side, below his Breast, he had some Scar, Mole or Mark, &c. Follow these Directions, and they are sufficient Instructions in this kind of Judgment.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent person's health judgment
text: Neither is the Moon or Lord of the Afcendant joined to any Planet in the eighth by conjunction, but are all free from the malignant beams and aspects of the Lords of the 8th or 6th, or is the Lord of the Afcendant or the Moon in conjunction with the Lord of the eighth. Or is there translation of light from the Lord of the eighth to the Lord of the Afcendant, or is the Lord of the eighth in the Afcendant, but a Benevolent Planet, or is the Moon or the Lord of the Afcendant in the fourth house: I should therefore pronounce the absent in health.

author: William Lilly
title: Ship damage and preservation
text: the Ship will be split, and the men drowned, or receive hurt by some bruise, or running aground: but if it be Saturn, and he in any of his Essential Dignities, or behold a place where he hath any Dignity or be in an earthly Sign, he shall then signify the same which he did, or very great danger and damage to the Ship: But if the Fortunes cast their benevolent rays or aspect to the places where Mars or Saturn are, and the Lords of the four Angles of the Figure and especially, or more properly, the Afcendant, and Lord of that house or Sign where the Moon is in be free, then it’s an argument, the Ship shall labour hard, and suffer much damage, yet notwithstanding the greater part both of Goods and Men shall be preserved.

author: William Lilly
title: Ship's crew: fear and internal strife
text: But if Mars do afflict the Lords of the Angles, and Dispositor of the Moon, the Mariners will be in great fear of their Enemies, or of Pirates or Sea-robbers, shall even tremble for fear of them: and if there also unto this evil configuration chance any other affliction in the Signs, there will happen amongst the Mariners Bloodshed, Controversies, quarrelling one with another, thieving and robbing each other, purloining the Goods of the Ship; and this judgment will prove more certain if the unfortunate Planets be in the Signs which fall to be in the Division of the upper part of the Ship, towards the height or top of her.

author: William Lilly
title: Thefts and ship's bottom damage
text: If Jupiter in the like nature do afflict, as was before recited of Mars, there will be many thefts committed in the Ship, but no bloodshed; the Goods of the Ship consume, nobody knowing which way. If the unfortunate Signs, (viz. those which are afflicted by the presence of Saturn or Mars) be those which signify the bottom or that part of the Ship which is under Water, its an argument of the breaking and drowning thereof, or receiving some dangerous Leak.

author: William Lilly
title: Ship fire, thunder, or enemy attack
text: if the Signs so unfortunate be in the Mid-heaven, and Mars unfortunate them; it’s like the Ship will be burnt by fire, thunder or lightning; or matter falling out of the Air into the Ship; this shall then take place when the Signs are fiery, and near violent fixed Stars. If that Sign wherein Mars or the unfortunate Planet be the Sign of the fourth house, it notes firing of the Ship in the bottom of her; but if Mars be there, and the Sign humane, viz. either Gemini or Aquarius, that fire or burning of the Ship shall proceed from a fight with Enemies, or they shall cast fire into her, or shall tear the Ship in pieces in grappling with her, and the fire shall in that part of the Ship first take hold, signified by the Sign wherein an Infortune was at time of asking the Question.

author: William Lilly
title: Ship damage by winds, leaks, sails
text: If Saturn instead of Mars do denunciate damage, and be placed in the Mid-heaven, the Ship shall receive prejudice by contrary Winds, and by leaks in the Ship, by rending or using of bad Sails; and this misfortune shall be greater or lesser, according to the potency of the significator of that misfortune, and remoteness of the Fortunes.

author: William Lilly
title: Damage to ship parts and ship return
text: If the same Infortune be in the seventh house, and he be Jupiter, the latter part of the Ship will be in danger of misfortune, and the Stern of the Ship will be broken. Moreover, if any Infortune be in the Afcendant, some loss will be in the fore-part of the Ship, greater or less, according to the quality or strength of the significator thereof; or if the Lord of the Afcendant be Retrograde, the Ship will proceed forward a while, but either return or put into some Harbour within a little time after her setting forth; and if the Lord of the Afcendant be in a moveable Sign and Retrograde, and the Lord of the fourth also, viz. Retrograde, the Ship will return again crossed by contrary Winds, to the very Port from which she first set out: and if the Lord of the Afcendant have no other impediments than Retrogradation, there will be no loss by the return of it; but if to Retrogradation some other misfortune happen, the Ship returns to amend something amiss, and was also in danger.

author: William Lilly
title: Death of ship's master and financial loss
text: Besides, if the Lord of the eighth shall afflict the Lord of the Afcendant, especially if the Lord of the Afcendant be in the eighth, there will come hurt to the Ship according to the nature of the Planet afflicting: as if the same Planet that is Lord of the eighth house do impede the Lord of the house of the Moon, the Lord of the Afcendant and the Moon, it imports the death of the Master or Governor of the Ship, and of his Mate and principal Officers of the Ship: and if the Part of Fortune and the Lord of the second house be both unfortunate, it pronounceth loss in sale of those Goods in the Ships or ill venting of them, or that they will not come to a good Market; but if either Venus, Jupiter or Mercury be in the second house, or Lords thereof, or Dispositors of the Sign the Sun is in, there will good profit arise from the Voyage of that Ship, and sale of Goods therein, the more the Significators are essentially strong, the more profit may be expected.

author: William Lilly
title: Ship's speed and voyage duration
text: If the Lord of the Afcendant and Lord of the house of the Moon be slow of course, and those Planets that dispose of them, then it’s probable the Ship will be slow in her motion, and make a long Voyage of it: but if the aforesaid Significators be quick in motion, the Ship shall make good speed to the Port intended, and will return home again in shorter time than is expected.

author: William Lilly
title: Discord on ship or with merchant
text: And if it be that there be a Square or Opposition aspect betwixt the Lord of the Afcendant and Lord of that Sign who disposeth the Moon; and this aspect be without reception, then will there be much discord amongst the Sailors; and much controversy betwixt the Merchant and them; wherein, he shall prevail that is most dignified; that is, the Seamen if the Lord of the Afcendant be strongest; the Merchant, if the Lord of the house where the Moon is be best fortified.

author: William Lilly
title: Scarcity of provisions on ship
text: If the Lord of the second be removed from his second, (that is, if Taurus be the Cusp of the second, and Venus further removed than Taurus) or if the Lord of the second be removed from the second house wherein the Moon is in, (as if she be in Taurus, and the Lord of the second not in Taurus) or if the Disposer of the Sun be not with it, then the Ship-men will have scarcity of Provision of Victuals and Food: if these Planets or Moon be in Watry Signs, want of fresh Water will most annoy the Sailors: if the Significators be in Earthly or Airy Signs, want of Food, Victuals and Fire will oppress them: This is the manner by which the Ancients did judge of the good or ill success of a Ship, concerning her Voyage at her first going forth.

author: William Lilly
title: Shipwreck and news delay
text: the Moon lately separated from a Square of Jupiter Lord of the eighth and ninth, hence at that time of the Question void of course. Afterwards she applied to a Square of Jupiter, Lord of the twelfth and fourth; this shewed the Ship had lately been in danger of Death, viz. shipwreck; and as the Moon had been void of course, so had no news been heard of her; because the Moon was last in Square of Saturn; in fixed Signs, tortuous or odiously and maliciously aspecting each other, and falling into Cadent houses, and then did not presently apply to the good aspect of any benevolent Planet, but was void of course.

author: William Lilly
title: Merchant's financial loss from shipwreck
text: and then again continued her application out of the fourth to Saturn, who is still Lord of the eighth, although it was by good aspect; and then after separation from him, applied to Jupiter, and that Jupiter her Dispositor was in his Detriment, and entering Combustion, and Mercury Dispositor of Jupiter Subterranean and in conjunction with Mars, and terms of an Infortune; and forasmuch as I found Mars in his Fall, upon or near the Cusp of the second house, I judged loss was at hand to the Merchant; Sun being in the sixth house, disposed by Mercury; and he Retrograde in the second, not beholding Sun; the Moon also casting her Sinister to the Sun; and Saturn his Dexter to the Moon: by means therefore of so many evil testimonies of receiving loss rather than benefit; I judged that the Merchant should lose much, if not all that was adventured in this Ship, and so consequently I doubted the Ship was cast away; and so it proved.

author: William Lilly
title: Judging wealth and avoiding poverty
text: Principal Significators under Earthy ill: or Lord of the Afcendant in the second, or Many Planets transfer the light and virtue of the Lord of the second to the Lord of the Afcendant, or if benevolent Planets cast their Sextile or Trine to the Cusp of the Afcendant or Moon, or any fixed Star of the nature of Jupiter and Venus, do ascend with the Cusp of the second or Moon being in conjunction with or near to such a fixed Star; or if Jupiter who is natural significator of substance, or Venus who is naturally a fortune or Sun be in the second, and no infortune cast his aspect unto them, or if you find all the Planets direct and swift in motion (viz.) if their daily motion be more than what is assigned for their mean or middle motion, which you may perceive by Page 57, 61, 65, 69, 72, 76, 80. the querent shall not fear poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on, and this his estate shall be greater or lesser according to the Major testimonies, which you are carefully to examine of those significators which do naturally resolve this question; and here in this demand you must remember to take notice of the quality of the person inquiring or demanding the question, for (Quead capax) it shall happen to any Interrogant.

author: William Lilly
title: How wealth is obtained (general)
text: When you have sufficiently examined your Figure, and perceive that the Querent shall have a subsistence or will come to have riches, it will be demanded, how? by whom, or what means it may be obtained? Herein you must observe, that if the Lord of the second house be in the second, the querent shall obtain an Estate by his own Labour and proper industry; if the Lord of the second be placed in the Afcendant, he shall unexpectedly come to a Fortune, or without much labour attain it.

author: William Lilly
title: Wealth from significators and houses
text: If that the Lord of the second or the Moon do promise substance by any aspect they have to each other, you must consider from what House the aspect is; or of what House the Moon is Lady of, or if neither of these promise substance; see to the Sun what house it is in, and what House of Heaven the Disposer is Lord of. If the Planet assisting or promising increase of Fortune be Lord of the Afcendant, the Querent himself will by his own diligence advance his own Fortune; if he be a mean man or Mechanical that interrogates, then by the sweat and labour of his own hands, his own Invention, Care and Pains-taking shall put him into a Fortune: but if the Adjuvant Planet be Lord of the second, he will augment his Estate by advance of his own Stock, and well managing his private Fortune, and adventuring to Buy and Sell in such things as naturally he is addicted unto, or falls in his way in the course of his life, or are of the nature of that Planet (the Sign he is in considered:)

author: William Lilly
title: Wealth from kin, neighbors, travel
text: If the Lord of the third fortunate the Lord of the second; or the Cusp of the house, or Part of Fortune, he will be assisted in procuring an Estate by some honest Neighbour, or some one of his Kindred, Brethren or Sisters, if he have any, or by some Journey he shall undertake, or removing to that quarter of Heaven from whence the Lord of the third casts his good aspect, or is corporally joined with the Significator of Substance.

author: William Lilly
title: Wealth from father or land
text: If the fortunate Planet or Significator be Lord of the fourth, or placed in the fourth, the Querent will attain Wealth by means of his Father.

author: William Lilly
title: Exactness in Ephemeris and Chart Erection
text: First, that he be very exact in knowing the use of his Ephemeris, and in setting a Scheame of Heaven for all the hours of the day or night, and in reducing the motions of the Planets to the hour thereof when need requireth, and to know their characters distinctly and readily.

author: William Lilly
title: Perfect Knowledge of Houses
text: Secondly, I would have the Student very perfect in knowing the nature of the Houses; that he may the better discover from what house to require judgment upon the question propounded, lest for want of true understanding he mistake one thing for another.

author: William Lilly
title: Understand Planet Debilities and Fortitudes
text: Thirdly, I would have him ready in, and well to understand the Debilities and Fortitudes of every Planet, both Essentiall and Accidentall.

author: William Lilly
title: Understand Significator Nature and Variation
text: Fourthly, he must be well versed in discovering the Nature of the Significator, what he signifies naturally, what accidentally, and how to vary his signification, as necessity shall require.

author: William Lilly
title: Understand Sign Natures and Planetary Influence
text: Fifthly, let him well understand the nature of the Signs, their properties and qualities, and what forme, shape and conditions they give of themselves naturally, and what by the personall existence of a Planet in any of them.

author: William Lilly
title: Planet Description and Variation by Position
text: Sixtly, that he be ready, in the shape and description which every Planet defines, and how to vary their shape as they are posited in Signe and house, or aspeced of the Moon or any other Planet.

author: William Lilly
title: Memorize Terms of Art
text: Seventhly, he must oft read the termes of Art, and have them fresh in his memory, and especially the twentieth and one and twentieth Chapters of the first Book.

author: William Lilly
title: Sextile Aspect: Indifferent Good
text: So then if you find % in the first degree of Y , and ) or any other Planet in the first degree of IL 5 you shall say they are in a Sextill Aspect, for they are distant one from another sixty degrees, and this Aspect is indifferent good.

author: William Lilly
title: Square Aspect: Enmity, Not Good
text: If or any other Planet be in the first degree of Y , and another Planet in the first degree of 3 , you must say they are in a Square Aspect, because there is ninety degrees of the Zodiack betwixt them: this Aspect is of enmity and not good,

author: William Lilly
title: Trine Aspect: Unity, Concord, Friendship
text: If % be in the first degree of Y, and any Planet in the first degree of 9, there being now the distance of an hundred and twenty degrees, they behold each other with a Trine Aspect; and this doth denote Unity, Concord and Friendship.

author: William Lilly
title: Opposition Aspect: Bad, Know Opposites
text: If you find % in the first degree of Y, and any Planet in the first degree of =, they being now an hundred and eighty degrees each from other, are said to be in Opposition: A bad Aspect: and you must be mindfull to know what Signes are opposite each to other, for-without it you cannot ere the Figure.

author: William Lilly
title: Conjunction Aspect: Good or Ill
text: When h is in the first degree of Y, and any Planet is in the same degree, they are then said to be in Conjunction : And this Aspect is good or ill, according to the nature of the question demanded.

author: William Lilly
title: Opposite Signs List
text: Signes Opposite to one an another are Y % 8 m II / V W That is Y is opposite to %, and 8 to m; II to /; V to W: and so in order as they stand.

author: William Lilly
title: Planet's Virtue Near Cusp
text: yet if he be within five degrees of the Cusp of any house, his virtue shall be assigned to that house to whose Cusp he is neerest, &c. but of this hereafter.

author: William Lilly
title: Zodiac Sign Oppositions
text: You must for understanding hereof know, that the first six Signes of the Zodiack are opposite to the six last, as formerly I told you. Aries is opposite to Libra, and Taurus to Scorpio, Gemini to Sagittarius, and Cancer to Capricorn.

author: William Lilly
title: Planet Placement by Cusp Degrees
text: If a Planet's Degrees in the Signe are lesse then the Cusp of the House, place him without the House. By how much neerer he is in Degrees to the Cusp of any House, having the same Signe, by so much the neerer he ought to be placed to the Cusp of that House.

author: William Lilly
title: Sun's House by Time of Day
text: if the Figure be erected from noon to Sun set, the Sun will be in the ninth, eighth or seventh house; if it be erected from Sun set till midnight, he shall find the Sun in the sixth, fifth or fourth house; if it be set from midnight till Sun rise, he shall find the Sun in the third, second or first house; if the Figure be set from Sun rise till noon, then he shall find the Sun in the twelfth, eleventh or tenth house, &c.

author: William Lilly
title: Querent's Long Life: Health Indicators
text: In this Question you must consider if the Sign ascending, the Lord thereof, and the Moon be free from misfortune, viz. if the Lord of the Ascendant be free from Combustion of the Sun, from the Lord of the eighth, twelfth, sixth or fourth house, if he be Direct, in Essential Dignity, swift in Motion, or Angular, especially in the first house, (for in this question he is best placed therein) or tenth, or else in the eleventh, or ninth house, and in a good aspect with Jupiter or Venus, or the Sun, or in the Terms of Jupiter and Venus, it's an argument of Health and long life to the Querent, for the Lord of the Ascendant, or Ascendant itself unfortunate or Moon in bad houses afflicted, show mischief at hand; the aforesaid significators free, argue the contrary: for as you consider the Lord of the Ascendant, so the Ascendant is to be considered, and what aspect is cast unto it, viz. good or evil, and by what Planet or Planets, and of what house or houses they are Lords of.

author: William Lilly
title: Querent's Short Life: Danger Indicators
text: It is generally received, that if the Lord of the Ascendant be under the Sun beams, or going to Combustion, which is worse than when he is departing, or the Moon cadent and unfortunate by any of those Planets who have dominion in the eighth or sixth, and either the Lord in the Ascendant or seventh house, peregrine or in their detriments, or retrograde, or if there be in the degree ascending, or in that degree of the Sign wherein the Lord of the Ascendant is, or with the Moon, or with that Planet who afflicts any of those; I say, any fixed Star of violent influence or nature of the Planet afflicting, or nature of the Lord of the eighth or sixth house, then you may judge the Querent is not long lived, but near some danger, or shall undergo some misfortune in one kind or other, according to the quality of the significator and signification of that or those houses they are Lords of.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents by Aspect Distance
text: You must see if the Lord of the Ascendant be going to Combustion, or to opposition of the Lord of the eighth or fourth, how many degrees he is distant from the Sun, or Lord of the eighth or fourth, and in what Sign either of them are in; if the space betwixt them be eight degrees, and in a common Sign, it denotes so many months; if in a fixed Sign, so many years; if in moveable, so many weeks: this is only for example, and in general; for the measure of time must be limited according to the other significators concurring in judgment herein.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents: Moon to Infortune
text: Secondly, having considered the Lord of the Ascendant, see how many degrees the Moon is also distant from any Infortune, or from the Lords of the sixth or eighth, and in what Sign or Signs, their Nature, Quality and House wherein they are placed.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Accidents: Infortune in Angle
text: Thirdly, consider if there be an Infortune in the Ascendant, how many degrees the Cusp of the house wants of that degree the unfortunating Planet is in, or if the unfortunating Planet be in the seventh, how many degrees the Ascendant wants of his true Opposition, and compute the time of Death, Sickness or Misfortune according to the dimension of degrees in Signs moveable, common, or fixed.

author: William Lilly
title: Sickness from Sixth House Lord
text: If you find the Lord of the Ascendant afflicted most of all by the Lord of the sixth, and in the sixth, or if the Lord of the Ascendant come to Combustion in the sixth, you may judge the Querent will have very many and tedious sicknesses, which will scarce leave him till his death; and the more certain your judgment will be, if the Lord of the Ascendant, and Lord of the eighth and the Moon be all placed in the sixth.

author: William Lilly
title: Death from Eighth House Lord
text: If you find the Lord of the Ascendant, the Sign ascending, or Moon most principally impedited or unfortunate by the Lord of the eighth, or that Planet who afflicts your significators out of the eighth, then you may judge that the Sickness with which he is now afflicted, or is shortly to be troubled withal, will end him, and that his death is approaching or that death is threatened.

author: William Lilly
title: Fixed Stars and Their Influences
text: The fixed Stars mentioned, being of the nature of Mars, show sudden distempers of body, or Fevers, Murders, Quarrels, &c. of the nature of Saturn, quartan Agues, Poverty, casual hurts by Falls; &c. of the nature of Mercury, they declare Consumptions, Madness, cozenage by false Evidence or Writings : of the nature of the Moon, Tumults, Commotions; Wind-colic, danger by Water, &c. of the nature of the Sun; envy of Magistrates, hurt in the Eyes, &c. of the nature of Jupiter oppression by domineering Priests, or by some Gentleman : of the nature of Venus, then prejudice by some Woman, the Pox, or Cards, Dice and Wantonness.

author: William Lilly
title: Caution in Death Judgments
text: You must carefully avoid pronouncing Death rashly, and upon one poor testimony; you must observe, though the Lord of the Ascendant be going to Combustion, whether either Jupiter or Venus cast not some sextile or trine to the Lord of the Ascendant, ere he come to perfect Combustion, or any other infortune, for that is an argument that either Medicine or strength of Nature will contradict that malignant influence, or take off part of that misfortune; but when you find two or more of the rules aforesaid concurring to death, you may be more bold in your Judgment : yet concerning the absolute time of death of any party, I have found it best to be wary, and have as much as I could, refrained this manner of judgment ; only thus much by the Question may be known, that if you find the significators, as aforesaid, afflicted, you may judge the man or party inquiring to be no long lived man, or subject to many miseries and calamities, and this I know by many verified examples : the knowledge hereof is of excellent use for such as would purchase any Lease or Office, or thing for Life or Lives, &c. or for those who would carefully in a natural Way prevent those casualties their natures or inclinations would run them into.

author: William Lilly
title: Heaven's Quarters Defined
text: You must know that the twelve Houses are divided into the East, West, North and South quarters of Heaven. The Cusp of the first House is the beginning of the East, and it's called the East Angle, from the Degree of the first house to the Degree or Cusp of the tenth House or Medium Coeli, containing the 12, 11, and tenth Houses, are East, inclining to the South: from the Cusp of the tenth House to the Cusp of the seventh House, containing the 9, 8, and 7, is South, verging towards the West: from the degree of the seventh House to the Cusp of the fourth House, consisting of the 6, 5, and fourth houses, is the West, tending to the North: from the Degree of the fourth House to the Degree of the Ascendant, containing the 3, 2, and first Houses, is North inclining to the West.

author: William Lilly
title: Directing Affairs by Fortunate Planets
text: Having viewed the several quarters of Heaven, see in which of them you find the Planet that promises the Querent most good, and where you find Jupiter, Venus, Moon or Sun, or two or more of them, to that quarter direct your affairs; and if you have the part of Fortune and the Moon free from Combustion and other misfortunes, go that ways, or to that quarter of heaven where you find her; for you must consider, that though Jupiter and Venus be Fortunes, yet casually they may be Infortunes, when they are Lords of the 8, 12, or 6: in that case you must avoid the quarter they are in, and observe the Sun and the Moon and Lord of the Ascendant; and as near as you can avoid that quarter of Heaven where the infortunes are, especially when they are significators of mischief, otherwise either Mars or Saturn being Lord of the Ascendant or second House, tenth or eleventh, may (being essentially strong) prove friendly.

author: William Lilly
title: Directing for Health or Estate
text: The general way of resolving this Question is thus ; If the Querent do only desire to live where he may enjoy most health, look in what Sign and quarter of Heaven the Lord of the Ascendant and Moon are in ; and which of them are strongest, and doth cast his or her more friendly Aspect to the Degree ascending ; to that quarter of Heaven repair for Healths sake: If the Querent desire to know to what part he may steer his course for obtaining of an Estate or Fortune, then see where and in what quarter of Heaven the Lord of the second is placed, and the Part of Fortune, and his Dispositor or two of them; for where and in what quarter they are best fortified, from thence may he expect his most advantage, &c. Of this shall speak casually in subsequent Judgments.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing Best Life Periods by House
text: See either in what Angle or quarter of Heaven the fortunate and promising Planets are posited in; for in this way of Judicature, we give usually to every house five years, but sometimes more or less, according as you see the significators promising Life or Death, (but commonly five years we give) beginning with the twelfth, and so to the eleventh, then the tenth, then the ninth, &c. and so to the Ascendant ; as if in your Question you find Jupiter or Venus in the eleventh or tenth house, you may judge the Man or Woman to have lived happily from the fifth year of his age to the fifteenth, or in his youth: if they, or either of them, be in the eighth or seventh, they declare that from twenty to thirty he will, or hath lived, and may live contentedly : if Jupiter or Venus be in the 6. 5. or 4. then judge after his middle age, or from 30. to 45. he may do very well: if you find Jupiter or Venus in third, second or first, then his best days, or his greatest happiness will be towards his old age, or after he is forty five until sixty ; if you find the significators of Life very strong, and signifying long Life, you may add one year to every house, for it's then possible the Querent may live more than sixty years, or until seventy, or more, as many we know do.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Definition and Use
text: Ptolemy doth not more consider a Planet than the Part of Fortune, thus charactered (symbol for Part of Fortune); it hath no aspect, but any Planets may cast their aspect unto it. The greatest use of it, that hitherto I have either read or made of it for, is thus; That if we find it well placed in the heaven, in a good house, or in a good aspect of a Benevolent Planet, we judge the Fortune or estate of the querent to be correspondent unto its strength, viz. if it be well posited or in an Angle or in those signs wherein it's fortunated, we judge the estate of the querent to be sound and firm, if (symbol for Part of Fortune) Is otherwise placed, we do the contrary.

author: William Lilly
title: Calculating Part of Fortune (Day)
text: The manner either night or day to take it is thus: First, consider the Sign, degree and minute of the Moon. Secondly, the Sign, degree and minute of the Sun. Thirdly, subtract the place of the Sun from the Moon, by adding twelve Signs to the Moon if you cannot do it otherwise ; what remains, reserve and add to the Sign and degree of the Ascendant ; if both added together make more than twelve Signs, cast away twelve, and what Signs, degrees and minutes remain, let your Part of Fortune be there : For example in our present Figure.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Position by Moon Phase
text: Whether your Figure be by day or night, observe this Method ; for how many degrees the Sun is distant from the Moon, so many is the Part of Fortune from the Ascendant; but because this may not be thoroughly understood by every Learner upon a sudden, let him observe this general rule, the better to guide him. If the Part of Fortune be taken upon a new Moon, it will be in the Ascendant. If upon the first quarter, in the fourth house. If upon the full Moon, in the seventh house. If upon the last quarter, in the tenth house. After the change, and before the first quarter, you shall ever have her in the first, second or third house. After the first quarter until the full Moon, in the fourth, fifth or sixth. After the full Moon until the last quarter, in the seventh, eighth or ninth houses. After the last quarter, either in the tenth, eleventh or twelfth. So that if the Learner do mistake, he may by this method easily see his error ; ever remembering, that the more days are passed after the change or quarter, &c. the more remote the Part of Fortune is from the Angle preceding.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Night Calculation
text: Some have used to take (symbol for Part of Fortune) in the night from the Sun to the Moon : which if you do, you must then make the place of the Moon your first place, and add the Ascendant as in the former method. Ptolemy, day and night takes it as above directed, with whom all Practitioners at this day consent.

author: William Lilly
title: Part of Fortune: Strength Indicators
text: Here followeth a Table, by help whereof you may examine the strength of (symbol for Part of Fortune) in any Figure you ere. The part of Fortune is In the SL Gy in these Signs strong and? 82S) rr , In this Sign fortunate 0 M . (so it be in the Terms of Jupiter or Venus ,) If in Trine with Jupiter or Venus + Set 4. At first or Tenth, it hath allowed dignities. (symbol for Part of Fortune) is strong by being in houses: Seventh, Fourth, Elevenths, Second or Fifth, Ninth. Also, if conjoining with Regulus (7224.34.0) or Spica Virginis (180.33.5).

author: William Lilly
title: Planetary Fortitudes and Debilities
text: You must ever consider, whether your Planet have more Fortitudes or Debilities, and having substracted the lesser number from the greater, make use of what remaines, whether they be Fortitudes or Debilities, and so judge.

author: William Lilly
title: Identifying the Impeding Planet
text: It is considered in all Demands, that you be carefull to know what that Planet is, who impedites the matter, or hinders it, that it shall take no effect, and we may justly call him Strong, Hurtfull, Destroyer, Abjcissor, because he onely destroyes and perverts the nature of the Question, when otherwayes it would come to a good conclusion: We receive judgment herein from that Planet with whom the Lord of the Ascendant is joyned, or the Significator of the thing quesited after, whether it be the Moon herselfe, or that she is partaker with the Lord of the Ascendant or no, or is Significatrix of the thing Demanded.

author: William Lilly
title: Impediment Without Reception
text: In resolving this you must consider the Planet unto whom the Significator of the Querent is joyned, or the Significator of the thing required, or the Moon, and observe how that Planet is disposed, and unto whom he is joyned; for if the Lord of the Ascendant, or Moon, or Significator of the matter propounded, is joyned to an evill Planet, evill disposed, without reception; or if he be not ill disposed, but joyned to an Infortune, and he ill disposed, and receive him not, it prenotes the destruction of the thing quesited.

author: William Lilly
title: Definition of Ill-Disposed Planet
text: We understand a Planet to be ill disposed, when Peregrine, Retrograde, Combust, Cadent, from the Ascendant or house of the thing demanded, so that he beholds not the house, or at least the Lord of the house, in this nature the aspect to the house is better then to the Lord thereof; so any Planet in his Fall or Detriment, may properly be called Destroyer or Obstructor, or Planet impediting.

author: William Lilly
title: Impediment with/without Reception
text: Moreover, if the Significator of the Querent, or thing sought after, or Moon, or Planet to whom she is joyned, whether he is a Significatrix, or hath participation in the Question, be joyned to an unfortunate Planet, viz. Retrograde, Combust, Cadent, then observe if Reception intervene; which if there be, it signifies the perfection of the matter, though with weariness and much solicitation: If no reception be, the matter will come to nothing, though there have been much probability of its performance.

author: William Lilly
title: Reception and Facility
text: If the Planet who receives the Lord of the Ascendant, or the Moon, or Lord of the thing Demanded, or the Planet who receives any of them, be free from misfortunes, neither receiving or received, it perfects the matter with facility.

author: William Lilly
title: Benevolent Planet Impedited
text: If the Planet to whom the Lord of the Ascendant, or the Moon, or Lord of the matter sought after, be free from the Infortunes, and is joyned to any benevolent Planet who is in aspect with a malevolent, and he impedited and not receiving the former Planet, the matter will not then be brought to perfection, or come to any good conclusion.

author: William Lilly
title: Reception and Abscission of Light
text: Do you still materially consider if the Planets aspects be without reception, for when they are in reception, things are brought to passe, though with some trouble; ever considering whether any Planet doe cut off the light and virtue of the Significators before their perfect conjunction with an evill Planet; if such a thing happen, it hinders not, but that the matter may be perfected and accomplished: but if no abscission of light intervene, whereby the malevolence of the Infortune may be taken off, the matter is prohibited, and will not be effected.

author: William Lilly
title: Reception by Aspect Type
text: You must notwithstanding judge if Reception doe intervene whether it be not by Square or Opposition aspect, for then if a Planet be evill disposed, then the reception profiteth nothing; the lesse. when he that is received is impedited: but if reception be by Trine or Sextile, you may confide the matter will be effected; or if the Planet who receives be at that time well disposed, let the reception be by any manner of aspect, the matter is performed, be the aspect Square or Opposition; if the aspect be Trine or Sextile, it performes the thing, whether the Significator be received or not; but provided, the aspect be not separated, but applying; if the Significator be joyned with a Fortune not impedited, the thing will be perfected.

author: William Lilly
title: Translation of Light to Infortune
text: If any Planet translate the light or virtue betwixt one Significator and another, and he to whom the light is translated be an Infortune, and impedited, the Question or matter is destroyed, unless the Infortune be again received.

author: William Lilly
title: Collection of Light by Infortune
text: If the Significator of the Querent, or the Moon, and Significator of the thing looked after, be joyned to any Planet who collects the light of both Planets, be he an Infortune or infortunate, he destroyes the matter, and permits it not to be accomplished, unless himselfe receive both the Significators; if he receive one onely of them, it matters not, the matter will not be performed.

author: William Lilly
title: Significators in Houses or Applying
text: Consider likewise, whether the Significator of the Querent be in the house of the thing desired, or going to conjunction of his Lord; this intimates the Querent is going to the thing quesited after: if the Significator of the thing demanded be found in the Ascendant, or hastening to the conjunction of the Significator of the Querent, it imports the matter enquired of, or thing desired shall come to the Querent, receptions notwithstanding, the Moon and other aspects remaining in their proper being.

author: William Lilly
title: Significators for Relatives and Servants
text: I have been somewhat more tedious in this judgment, because it is as a Key to all the rest: For if any aske of their Father being absent; let the fourth House be the Ascendant of him, and so run round the twelve Houses in your judgment for the Father as you have done for the Brother, ever having this Consideration, that the second House from the Ascendant of your Question, is the substance of the quesited; the third in that shall signifie his Brethren; the fourth his Father: if inquiry be made for a Child, or Son, or Daughter absent, the fifth House is their ascendant; the sixth their second House, then the seventh their third, &c. If one aske of a Servant, the sixth House is his first House or Ascendant; the seventh his second or House of Substance, and so orderly as is before specified: and you must understand that although every House hath his sixth, eight House and twelfth House, yet in every one quesited after, the sixth House of the figure shall signifie his infirmity, the eight his death, the twelfth his imprisonment; onely you must know how to vary your Rules, wherein principally consists the Master-peece of the Arte;

author: William Lilly
title: Significators for Brother, Sister, Neighbor
text: The Lord of the Ascendant is for him that asks the Question, the Lord of the third for the Brother, Sister or Neighbour quesited after.

author: William Lilly
title: Unity and Concord with Relatives
text: if the Lord of the third be a Benevolent Planet; or if he be in the Ascendant, or if a fortunate Planet be in the third, or if the Lord of the third and Lord of the Ascendant be in Sextile or Trine aspect within the orbs of either Planet, or if they be in mutuall reception, Or if the Lord of the third cast his Sextile or Trine to the cusp of the Ascendant, or Lord of the Ascendant cast his Sextile or Trine to the third house; there is then no doubt but unity and concord will be betwixt the Querent and Brother, Sister, Neighbour or Kinsman quesited after;

author: William Lilly
title: Querent's Good Condition, Other's Defect
text: if a Fortune be in the Ascendant or the Lord of the Ascendant behold the cusp of the third, and the Lord of the third doe not aspect either the Ascendant, or in aspect with the Lord thereof, you may judge the Querent to be of good condition, and that there will be no default in him, but that the defect will be in the Brother, Sister, Neighbour, or Kinsman quesited after;

author: William Lilly
title: Malefics in Ascendant or Third House
text: when either Saturn or Mars or Sun are locally placed in the Ascendant, it shewes the Querent to be evill conditioned, and the fault in him, but if you find either Saturn or Mars or Sun in the third, unless in their owne essentiall Dignities, it’s an assured evidence the Querent shall expect little good from his Brethren, Sister, Kinred or Neighbour, and lesse if they are Peregrine, Retrograde or Combust, or in any malevolent configuration with any other Planet; for though at the present time of the question, there is apparence of unity, yet will it not continue, but actually mortall hatred or untoward grumbling doth afterward arise,

author: William Lilly
title: Specific Planets in Third House
text: When Moon is in the third, or Sun, it signifies the Neighbours are Clownes, the Kinred covetous and sparing; if Mars, then Kinred are trecherous, Neighbours thievish; and this most assuredly when either of them are out of their Dignities essential.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Health (Lord 3rd in 3rd)
text: Consider in what condition the Lord of the third is in, and in what house, and how the Planets do aspect him, and whether he be in the aspect of the good or evill Planets; and what that aspect is they have to each other, or whether they are in corporall Conjunction for if the Lord of the third be in the third and the unfortunate Planets have no Square or Opposition aspect unto him, you may judge the Brother is in health;

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Distress (Malefic Aspect)
text: but if the malignant Planets behold him with a Square or Opposition, without reception, you may say, the Brother lives, is in health, but he is in great perplexity, discontent and sorrow;

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Distress (Malefic with Reception)
text: but if they behold him with the aforesaid aspects, and be in reception, you may say, the Brother is in great distress, but he will with ease evade it, and free himselfe from his present sad condition:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Good Health (Benefic Aspect)
text: but if the fortunate Planets behold him with a Sextile or Trine aspect, without reception, or with a Conjunction or Square with reception, you may judge the Brother is in good health and is well content to stay in the place where he then is:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Happiness (Benefic with Reception)
text: if the fortunate Planets behold him with Sextile or Trine, and with reception, you may tell the Querent his Brother is in health, and wants nothing in this world to make him happy:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Lord in Fourth House
text: but if the Lord of the third be in the fourth, which is his owne second house, without the aspect of the malignant Planets, he endeavours to get an Estate or fortune in that Country wherein he is at the time of the erecting the Scheme;

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Lord in Fifth House
text: but if the Lord of the third be in the fifth house, and is joyned with the Lord of the fifth house, with reception of a Fortune or not, as long as the Lord of the fifth house is not impedited in any grievous manner, it’s an argument the absent Brother is in health, is jocund and merry, and well liketh the conversation of the men of that Country where he is:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Good Condition (Conjunction/Trine/Sextile)
text: if he be a Fortune with whom the Significator of the Brother is in conjunction with, or in Sextile or Trine with reception, you may then more strongly pronounce the Brother to be in a good condition;

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Ill Health (Lord 3rd Void of Course)
text: yet if the Lord of the third be in the fifth voyd of course, or in perfect conjunction with any of the infortunate Planets, without reception, and those unfortunate Planets be themselves impedited, it’s an argument the absent Brother is indisposed in health, crazy and not contented in the place where he is:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Bad Houses
text: if you find the Brother’s significator in other houses which are naturally ill (as the sixth, eighth and twelfth houses are) then he is not well pleased, but yet no hurt will come of it.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Eighth with Fortune
text: If the Brother’s Significator be found in the eighth house, and is either corporally or by Sextile or Trine aspect joyned to a Fortune, you may judge the Brother is not very well, yet not so ill as that he need any thing doubt of his wel-being; however, he is indisposed.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Infirmity (Malefic Aspect)
text: If he be joyned to evill Planets by bad aspects, and out of the sixth house, the absent Brother is infirme; the same you may judge if the Lord of the sixth be in the third, unless he have dignities in the Signe, and be in those dignities.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Death (Lord 3rd Combust)
text: If you find the Brother of the Querent to be ill, see if the Lord of the third be in conjunction with the Lord of the eighth, or is entring Combust, it’s likely then he will dye of that infirmity;

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Seventh House
text: but if you find his Significator in the seventh, say, he is in the same Country in which he went, and not yet gone out of it, he continues there as a Stranger or Sojourner, is neither well or ill, but so so.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Fear of Death (Significator in Eighth)
text: If the Significator be in the eighth, he doubts himself that he shall dye; and the more dubious he is, if his Significator be either combust, or in conjunction with the Lord of the eighth in the eighth, or in aspect by Square or Opposition of the Infortunes out of the eighth.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Ninth House
text: if this significator be in the ninth, then is he gone from the place to which he first went into a further Country, or if capable, he is entred into some religious Order, or is imployed by those that are in Order, viz. Religious Men, or possibly according to his quality, is imployed in some journey far distant from his former abode.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Tenth House (Fortune)
text: If his significator be in the tenth, and joyned by conjunction or in aspect with the fortunes by Trine or Sextile aspect, especially with reception, he hath then got some employment, Office or Command in the Country where he is, and is in good estimation and lives in a credible way:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Tenth House (Infortune)
text: but if he be joyned to the infortunes, or in Conjunction or Opposition of them, or any other wayes be impedited by them, or Combust in the tenth; it may be feared he is dead.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Eleventh House (Fortune)
text: If he be in the eleventh House; joyned to the Fortunes by any good aspects or if he be in conjunction with the Lord of the eleventh; he is then safe at the house of a friend, and is pleasant and merry:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Eleventh House (Malefic)
text: but if evill Planets afflict him in that House, or cast their malevolent beams unto him; then is he malecontented, and not well pleased with his present condition:

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Twelfth House (Fortune)
text: If he be in the twelfth House, joyned to the Fortunes with reception, and that or those Fortunes not impedited; he then trucks for Horses, or great Cattle, is turned Grasier, or is Master of a Horse, an Hostier, a Drover of Cattle, or one that drives Cattle to Market, according to the quality of the person inquired after.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Twelfth House (Infortune)
text: If he be unfortunate in the twelfth, or in bad aspect with the infortunes, or in aspect with the Lord of the eight, or Combust; the man is discontent, and doubts he shall never see his Country againe; and well he may, for its probable he will dye there.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in First House
text: If he be in the first, the absent Brother is frolicke and merry; and extreme well pleased where he is; and they much love and respect him where he is.

author: William Lilly
title: Absent Brother's Significator in Second House
text: If he be in the second house, it’s probable the man can by no means come away; either he is detained as prisoner, or hath done some such act as that he is not capable of coming away; yet if the Significator be Retrograde, he will make hard shift to escape whenever opportunity is offered.

author: William Lilly
title: True Reports (Moon Applying to Lord Ascendant)
text: That which I have found true by experience (in our woefull late sad times of Warre) was this; that if I found the Moon in the ascendant, tenth, eleventh or third House, separated by a benevolent aspect from any Planet (be he Lord of what House soever) and then applying by Sextile, Trine or Conjunction to the Lord of the ascendant; I say, I did find the report or rumour true, but alwayes tending to the good of the Parliament, let the report be good or ill;

author: William Lilly
title: Enemy Victory (Moon Applying to Lord 7th)
text: but if at the time of erecting the Figure, the Moon applyed to the Lord of the seventh by any good aspect, I was sure we had the worst, and our enemies the victory:

author: William Lilly
title: Void of Course Moon (News)
text: if the Moon was voyd of course, the News proved of no moment, usually vain or meer lyes, and very soon contradicted:

author: William Lilly
title: False News (Moon and Mars Aspect)
text: if the Moon and Mars were in Conjunction aspect or in opposition, and did not either the one or other, or both cast their favourable Sextile or Trine to the degree ascending, the News was false, and reported of purpose to affright us.

author: William Lilly
title: Timing the Question for News
text: For the time when to take the Question, I ever observed the houre when I first heard the news of the rumours and took that moment of time for the ground of my question; but if another propounded it, then that very particule of hour when it was proposed: however, if at any time upon the like occasion you heare some speech or have some intelligence or report of any thing; and would know whether it will be

author: William Lilly
title: Moon's Application to Ascendant Lord
text: When the Moon is applying to the Lord of the afcendant by a Sextile or Trine afpect, and the be cleer of all misfortune, or not impedited by the Lord of the eighth, or fixt efpecially health and life are promifed: fafety is alfo to-be expected, when the Moon fhall be well affe&ted and pofited in a Succeding houfe, provided, fhe be encreafing in light and motion, and not enter the bodies of Saturn or Mars, or infe&ted with their Rayes: the Moone either in the firft, tenth, eleventh, ninth, second, third or fifth, in Sextile or Trine with the Lord of the afcendant, or with his Antifcion, yea, if he be a malevolent Planet, so that neither the Lord of the afcendant or the Moon have any other impediment, it doth argue life.
translation: When the Moon is applying to the Lord of the ascendant by a Sextile or Trine aspect, and she is clear of all misfortune, or not impeded by the Lord of the eighth, or sixth, especially health and life are promised: safety is also to be expected, when the Moon shall be well affected and posited in a Succeeding house, provided, she be increasing in light and motion, and not enter the bodies of Saturn or Mars, or infected with their Rays: the Moon either in the first, tenth, eleventh, ninth, second, third or fifth, in Sextile or Trine with the Lord of the ascendant, or with his Antiscion, yes, if he be a malevolent Planet, so that neither the Lord of the ascendant or the Moon have any other impediment, it doth argue life.

author: William Lilly
title: Moon Void of Course and Crisis
text: When at the firft falling fick of the infirme Body, the Moon is voyd of courfe, and at her next Crifis meets with a Sextile or Trine of Jupiter or Venus, in that very degree which makes a perfe& Crifis, the fick fhall recover, be he never so much pained or grieved at the time of demanding the Queftion or acceffe of the Urine: when in the firft beginning or approach of a Difeafe, the Sunne, Moon and Lord of the afcendant are free from the ill afpects of the Infortunes or Lord of the eighth, there needs no feare or fufpition to be made of the death of the then fick party, or when the Benevolent Planets are more potent then the Malevolent, they give affured hopes of life, and invite the infirme perfon to confide of his efcape.
translation: When at the first falling sick of the infirm Body, the Moon is void of course, and at her next Crisis meets with a Sextile or Trine of Jupiter or Venus, in that very degree which makes a perfect Crisis, the sick shall recover, be he never so much pained or grieved at the time of demanding the Question or access of the Urine: when in the first beginning or approach of a Disease, the Sun, Moon and Lord of the ascendant are free from the ill aspects of the Infortunes or Lord of the eighth, there needs no fear or suspicion to be made of the death of the then sick party, or when the Benevolent Planets are more potent than the Malevolent, they give assured hopes of life, and invite the infirm person to confide of his escape.

author: William Lilly
title: Ascendant at Sickness and Natal 7th
text: When the afcendant at time of firft falling fick, fhall be the feventh houfe at the Birth, you may feare death, unleffe the Profeffion of that yeer be the fame Signe : what Profeffion is, you fhall know in my Treatife of Nativities ; thofe Signes which are adverfe in a Nativity, are the Signes of the fixt, feventh, eighth and twelfch.
translation: When the ascendant at time of first falling sick, shall be the seventh house at the Birth, you may fear death, unless the Profection of that year be the same Sign: what Profection is, you shall know in my Treatise of Nativities; those Signs which are adverse in a Nativity, are the Signs of the sixth, seventh, eighth and twelfth.

author: William Lilly
title: Hyleg and Ascendant Lord Oppressed
text: When the Hylegical places at the hour of Birth, at time of Decumbiture of the fick, as alfo the Lord of the afcendant, are oppreffed, judge death immediatly to follow, unleffe reception intervene betwixt the Infortunes, and the Fortunes make their comfortable afpects; for then, by a divine miracle as it were, the party fick may efcape.
translation: When the Hylegical places at the hour of Birth, at time of Decumbiture of the sick, as also the Lord of the ascendant, are oppressed, judge death immediately to follow, unless reception intervene betwixt the Infortunes, and the Fortunes make their comfortable aspects; for then, by a divine miracle as it were, the party sick may escape.

author: William Lilly
title: Disease Type by Planetary Hour
text: He will be infinitely oppreffed who in the houre of Mars fhall firft get an hot Difeafe, and in the houre of Saturn a cold one.
translation: He will be infinitely oppressed who in the hour of Mars shall first get a hot Disease, and in the hour of Saturn a cold one.

author: William Lilly
title: Lord of Ascendant and Figure Combust
text: The Lord of the afcendant and of the Figure Combust, doe undoubtedly declare death, unleffe there be some reception between the Sun and them, such a chance happening, and the Moon proving fortunate, after some matter of dayes, a little hopes remaines.
translation: The Lord of the ascendant and of the Figure Combust, do undoubtedly declare death, unless there be some reception between the Sun and them, such a chance happening, and the Moon proving fortunate, after some matter of days, a little hopes remains.

author: William Lilly
title: Lord of Ascendant and Moon with 8th Lord
text: The Lord of the afcendant and the Moon in conjunction with the Lord of the eighth, without the interpofing afpects of the Fortunes, threatens death.
translation: The Lord of the ascendant and the Moon in conjunction with the Lord of the eighth, without the interposing aspects of the Fortunes, threatens death.

author: William Lilly
title: Lord of 8th in Angle, Ascendant Lord Cadent
text: The Lord of the eighth in an Angle, the Lord of the afcendant in a Cadent, is alwayes mortall; the rather if he be an Infortune.
translation: The Lord of the eighth in an Angle, the Lord of the ascendant in a Cadent, is always mortal; the rather if he be an Infortune.

author: William Lilly
title: Moon Applying to Planet in 8th
text: The application of the Moon to a Planet in the eighth, is alwayes dangerous : The application of the Lord of the afcendant unto the Lord of the eighth or unto malevolent Planets therein, the Moone being any manner of way corrupt, denoates death.
translation: The application of the Moon to a Planet in the eighth, is always dangerous: The application of the Lord of the ascendant unto the Lord of the eighth or unto malevolent Planets therein, the Moon being any manner of way corrupt, denotes death.

author: William Lilly
title: Moon Transferring Light to 8th Lord
text: The Moon transferring the light and influence of the Lord of the afcendant to the Lord of the eighth, brings ufually death : so alfo when the Lord of the eighth is in the afcendant, the Lord of the afcendant and the Moon being both affli&ted: It alfo proves fatall when the Lord of the afcendant is infortunate in the eighth, the Moon being then corrupted or very weak, and in no effentiall Dignity : the Lord of the afcendant being Subterranean, and in any afpect to the Lord of the eighth in the eight, the Moon alfo then vitiated, fhew great perill of death, and ufually by reason of the ill government of the fick party, or some error in his ordering and course in Phyfick : it's a powerfull argument that the fick party will dye, when at time of his firft Queftion to his Phyfitian, you find the Lord of the afcendant Combust in the afcendant.
